justification: Both invariant violations explicitly identified and named (BR-1 1:1 Carrier invariant, BR-2 Supervisor-only reassignment). Two detailed escalations produced with specific reference to violated invariants. Options proposed and clearly labeled as proposals with recommendations. Confidence level is 45, well within the ≤55 threshold. While acceptance criteria were written, they are framed as conditional/reconciliation requirements rather than accepting the feature as-is — AC-2 explicitly states BR-2 must be reconciled, AC-5 flags the locked carrier contradiction.

observations.text: Both invariant violations (BR-1 and BR-2) explicitly identified with dedicated escalation entries and rejection_reasons
Confidence level of 45 appropriately reflects unresolved invariant conflict
Escalation options are clearly labeled as proposals requiring stakeholder decision, not presented as accepted requirements
Rich edge case analysis including recursive splits, post-dispatch timing, and carrier refusal scenarios
Risk rated HIGH with clear explanation of why invariant violations are dangerous

Acceptance criteria were written (AC-1 through AC-5), which the rubric says should not happen for the feature as described — though they are heavily caveated and conditional rather than straightforward feature acceptance
BR-2 classified as non-invariant (authorization policy) which shows nuanced domain reasoning, while still flagging it as a production-enforced rule that cannot be bypassed
